item.title: Australia Inc starts to feel Iran war fallout, raising stagflation risk
item.description: Australia's corporates are starting to count the cost of the war in the Middle East, ​with profit warnings from two top companies and a crash in business sentiment pointing to pain from rising ‌prices, raising the risk of stagflation.

item.title: Exclusive: Chinese chipmaker YMTC plans new factories amid heightened US-Sino trade tensions, sources say
item.description: Chinese chipmaker Yangtze Memory Technologies (YMTC) aims to build two more factories ​in addition to one that will be completed this year, which will more than double its production capacity when ‌all three are up and running, people familiar with the plans said.

item.title: Wegovy-maker Novo Nordisk partners with OpenAI to speed drug development
item.description: Danish drugmaker Novo Nordisk , which has fallen behind Eli Lilly in the immensely lucrative weight-loss drug market, said on Tuesday it is partnering with OpenAI to deploy artificial intelligence across its business, from drug discovery to ​manufacturing and commercial operations.

item.title: From airlines to banks: Australian, New Zealand firms feel Gulf crisis heat
item.description: Companies in Australia and New Zealand are beginning to signal the financial strain from the U.S.-Israeli war on Iran, as higher fuel prices stoke inflation, dent business and consumer confidence, and weigh on corporate earnings.

item.title: Publicis posts 4.5% net revenue organic growth, maintains lead in US and Chinese markets
item.description: Publicis on Tuesday reported ‌first-quarter net revenue organic growth of 4.5%, meeting company-provided consensus and outpacing advertising industry peers, as AI and acquisitions drove gains in key markets, including the U.S. and China.

item.title: Italy's surprise rise in exports to US masks deep fragility to tariffs
item.description: Italy was the only major EU country whose exports to the United States grew last year despite Donald Trump's tariffs. The result was hailed by Rome's government, ​but a closer look at the data shows one-off factors at play and little underlying resilience.

item.title: After tackling Kering's debt problems, de Meo faces the Gucci challenge
item.description: Seven months into the job, Luca de Meo has bought himself time as CEO of Gucci owner Kering . This week he has to prove he ​knows how to use it.

item.title: Sazerac won’t easily gatecrash Jack Daniel's-maker's merger talks
item.description: Privately held bourbon maker Sazerac has emerged as an interloper in Pernod-Ricard's ​attempt to merge with Jack Daniel's owner Brown-Forman, according to a source familiar with the matter, even though analysts still think Pernod's portfolio ‌and global reach make the French spirits maker a better fit.

item.title: Morning Bid: Fingers crossed for a resolution
item.description: A look at the day ahead in European and global markets from Rae Wee

item.title: China Evergrande founder pleads guilty to fraud in Shenzhen court
item.description: The founder of China Evergrande Group, the world's ​most indebted property developer, pleaded guilty to charges ‌including misuse of funds, fundraising fraud and illegally taking public deposits, a court in China's southern city of Shenzhen said.

item.title: Korean AI chip startup DeepX prepares public share offering
item.description: South Korean AI chip ​startup DeepX said ‌on Tuesday it is preparing to ​list its shares ​domestically, adding the firm ⁠is open ​to a possible U.S. ​listing after that.

item.title: OpenAI's $852 billion valuation faces investor scrutiny amid strategy shift, FT reports
item.description: OpenAI's $852 billion valuation is under scrutiny from some of its own backers ​as the company shifts its focus to the enterprise ‌market to fend off competition from Anthropic, the Financial Times reported on Tuesday.

item.title: Bessent says Fed should 'wait and see' before lowering rates, Semafor reports
item.description: The Federal Reserve should "wait and see" before ​deciding whether to lower interest rates amid the war ‌in Iran, U.S. Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent told Semafor Editor-in-Chief Ben Smith on Monday.

item.title: China's export engine stutters as Iran war wipes out AI-driven gains
item.description: China's export engine slowed ​in March as buyers chasing an AI-fuelled future ran into the hard reality of war in the Middle East, ‌which has sparked an energy shock and complicated Beijing's push to keep growth on track.

item.title: Amazon nears deal to buy Globalstar, Bloomberg News reports
item.description: Amazon is in ​advanced talks to ‌acquire satellite telecom group ​Globalstar , a deal that ​would bolster the tech major's push to ⁠build its ​own satellite ​operation, Bloomberg News reported on Monday.

item.title: Stocks gain, oil and dollar retreat on hopes for US-Iran resolution
item.description: Asian stocks advanced while oil prices and the safe-haven dollar fell on Tuesday as the U.S. said it continued to engage with Tehran to make a ​deal even as it blocked Iran's ports after the collapse of peace talks over the weekend.

item.title: Nissan to streamline global lineup to 45 models from 56
item.description: Nissan Motor said ​on Tuesday it plans ‌to streamline its global lineup to 45 ​models from ​56 and seek to deploy ⁠its artificial ​intelligence driving technology across ​90% of its lineup over the long term.

item.title: Australian business sentiment crashes in March on worries about fallout from Iran war
item.description: Australian business confidence plunged last month by a magnitude only seen during financial crises as ​the Iran war gave rise to a global ‌oil shock, a survey showed on Tuesday.

item.title: Dollar steady as US blocks Iranian ships, talks continue
item.description: The dollar ​was largely steady on Tuesday as investors weighed supply risks stemming from a U.S. blockade of Iranian ‌ships in the Strait of Hormuz against the prospect of continued dialogue between Washington and Tehran that could lead to a breakthrough.

item.title: Exclusive: United CEO Kirby raised potential tie-up with American in Trump meeting
item.description: United Airlines CEO ​Scott Kirby raised ‌the potential of merging with American ​Airlines in ​a meeting with U.S. ⁠President Donald ​Trump in late ​February, two sources told Reuters.

item.title: Qantas lifts fuel cost forecast as Middle East war jolts oil markets
item.description: Australia's Qantas Airways said on Tuesday it has raised its fuel cost outlook and has not ​started its planned share buyback, citing sharply higher and volatile jet ‌fuel prices after the war in the Middle East cut oil supply.

item.title: Surging petrol prices drive record EV sales in Europe in March
item.description: High petrol prices in Europe steered car buyers towards EVs in a record-beating March, and led to the first month of global ​EV sales growth this year, data by consultancy Benchmark Mineral ‌Intelligence showed on Tuesday.

item.title: United Airlines CEO pitched possible combination with rival American, Bloomberg News reports
item.description: United Airlines CEO Scott Kirby has floated a ​possible combination with rival American Airlines ‌Group , Bloomberg News reported on Monday, citing people familiar with the talks.

item.title: Millions of Rockstar Games business records stolen, hacking group says
item.description: A hacking group with a history of compromising major global corporations said it had stolen nearly 80 million records ​from video game developer Rockstar Games, the maker of Grand ‌Theft Auto.

item.title: US crude futures fall over $1 as traders weigh supply risks
item.description: U.S. crude futures fell more ​than $1 in early ​Asian trade on Tuesday ⁠as traders balanced ​heightened supply risks linked ​to a U.S. blockade of Iranian shipping in the ​Strait of Hormuz ​with reports that Washington and Tehran ‌were ⁠still open to talks after a tense meeting in Islamabad.

item.title: Bloom Energy to supply up to 2.8 GW of fuel cells under expanded Oracle deal
item.description: Bloom Energy said on Monday it would supply Oracle with up to 2.8 ​gigawatts of fuel cell capacity under ‌an expanded deal as rising artificial intelligence use drives up power consumption.

item.title: Bending Spoons cuts Eventbrite staff, rolls out product changes after takeover
item.description: Eventbrite's new owner, Italian tech company Bending Spoons, said on Monday it had cut a large portion of the ​American ticketing platform's pre-acquisition workforce and implemented broad product ‌changes in the weeks following the deal closure.

item.title: FedEx CFO John Dietrich to step down
item.description: FedEx said on Monday Chief Financial ​Officer John Dietrich would step down, ‌effective June 1, after the parcel firm completes the spinoff of FedEx Freight into a publicly traded company.

item.title: TRADING DAY US stocks gain, dollar dips on hopes for Iran war negotiations
item.description: Wall Street's main indexes rallied to close sharply higher on Monday as hopes for de-escalation in the Middle East helped investors look past the failed U.S.-Iran talks and focus on the start of first-quarter earnings season.

item.title: Financial Stability Board chair warns of stability "triple whammy" as funding conditions tighten
item.description: The global financial system has so far absorbed the economic shock caused by the ​Iran war, partly thanks to post-2008 crisis ‌reforms, but rising funding costs may exacerbate growing stress in other parts of the system, the chair of ​the Financial Stability Board warned the G20 ​on Monday.
